data.text/markdown:
Connect to SQLite

You can use marimo's SQL cells to read from and write to SQLite databases. The first step is to attach a SQLite database. We attach to a sample database in a read-only mode below. For advanced usage, see duckdb's documentation.

data.text/html:
Create a SQL cell in one of two ways:
  1. Click the SQL button at the bottom of your notebook
  2. Right-click the button to the left of a cell, and choose SQL.
In the SQL cell, you can query dataframes in your notebook as if they were tables — just reference them by name.

<marimo-ui-element object-id='bkHC-0' random-id='63d0b379-fa60-308d-0c25-e367a28bf442'><marimo-table data-initial-value='[]' data-label='null' data-data='"[{\"table_name\":\"Album\"},{\"table_name\":\"Artist\"},{\"table_name\":\"Customer\"},{\"table_name\":\"Employee\"},{\"table_name\":\"Genre\"},{\"table_name\":\"Invoice\"},{\"table_name\":\"InvoiceLine\"},{\"table_name\":\"MediaType\"},{\"table_name\":\"Playlist\"},{\"table_name\":\"PlaylistTrack\"}]"' data-total-rows='11' data-total-columns='1' data-max-columns='50' data-banner-text='""' data-pagination='true' data-page-size='10' data-field-types='[["table_name",["string","str"]]]' data-show-filters='true' data-show-download='true' data-show-column-summaries='true' data-show-data-types='true' data-show-page-size-selector='true' data-show-column-explorer='true' data-show-chart-builder='true' data-row-headers='[]' data-has-stable-row-id='false' data-lazy='false' data-preload='false' data-download-file-name='"df"'></marimo-table></marimo-ui-element>

data.text/markdown: Once the database is attached, you can query it with SQL. For example, the next cell computes the average track length of each composer in the chinook database.

data.text/markdown:
You can explore the schemas of all your tables at a glance in the data sources panel: click the icon in the left sidebar to open it.

SQlite directly

Above we've been using in memory DuckDB to query Sqlite (which can be very performant) but you can also make a Sqlite connection directly.
The SQL cell below links to SQLite engine, so that's the backend that we will use. You can tell because we are using AVG instead of MEAN below.
